identifiers.source: Rossmann product URL for the 250 ml listing

sources.title: Rossmann JOHN FRIEDA Frizz Ease Wunder-Reparatur Conditioner
sources.evidence: Confirms brand, line, product name, conditioner category, 250 ml size, price 9.99 EUR, article number 214257, German-market listing, target hair type, color-treated suitability, ceramide repair positioning, vegan claim, usage instructions, and full ingredient list.

sources.title: Flaconi JOHN FRIEDA Frizz Ease Wunder-Reparatur RL Conditioner
sources.evidence: Confirms John Frieda Frizz Ease Wunder-Reparatur RL Conditioner, conditioner category, 250 ml option at 9.99 EUR, dry/unruly/dull hair positioning, ceramide repair claims, breakage reduction claim, vegan formula, application instructions, and matching ingredient list.

sources.title: Idealo John Frieda Frizz Ease Wunder-Reparatur Conditioner 250 ml
sources.evidence: Confirms 250 ml tube packaging, John Frieda Frizz Ease series, conditioner category, repair/moisture/anti-frizz effects, target hair types including dry, damaged, frizzy and unruly hair, and rinse-out application.

field_rationales.identifiers[0]: Rossmann's exact 250 ml product URL ends in /p/5037156298697, so this is treated as the GTIN-like product identifier for the submitted item.
field_rationales.identifiers[1]: Rossmann lists Artikelnummer 214257 on the exact 250 ml conditioner page.
field_rationales.product.image_url: Rossmann provides an exact product-only front packshot for the same 250 ml listing. It is clean enough for review/background removal, though the direct render URL exposed by the page is 600x600.
field_rationales.product.price_eur: Rossmann and Flaconi both show the 250 ml variant at 9.99 EUR at research time.
field_rationales.product.clean_name: After separating brand and line, the remaining product name is Conditioner; Rossmann and Flaconi both identify it as Wunder-Reparatur Conditioner.
field_rationales.product.category_key: The submission category is conditioner and Rossmann, Flaconi, and Idealo all categorize the product as a conditioner/rinse-out hair care product.
field_rationales.product.product_line: The repeated source identity is John Frieda Frizz Ease Wunder-Reparatur, so Frizz Ease Wunder-Reparatur is the product line.
field_rationales.product.affiliate_link: Rossmann is the clearest German retailer page for the exact 250 ml product and price.
field_rationales.product.canonical_brand: All sources use John Frieda as the brand.
field_rationales.product.purchase_link_status: Rossmann page was available and purchase-capable at research time.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_conditioner_specs: The product is positioned for stubborn, damaged, frizzy, dry hair and uses emollient/humectant/ceramide conditioning rather than protein repair, so it is best scoped to normal and coarse hair with moisture-need pull-test fit.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_conditioner_reran: The product has strong repair claims and ceramide positioning, but the formula is a daily conditioner rather than a mask; ingredient flags come from oils and humectants in the INCI.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_conditioner_specs: Include normal hair because the product is a standard rinse-out conditioner but has repair/anti-frizz conditioning weight.
Include coarse hair because sources explicitly position it for widerspenstiges, geschädigtes and krauses/frizzy hair.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_conditioner_reran: Medium because it contains cetearyl alcohol, isopropyl myristate, conditioning quats and oils, but it is still a daily rinse-out conditioner and not an intensive mask.
High because Rossmann and Flaconi emphasize Wunder-Reparatur, ceramides, damage repair, breakage reduction after one use and future damage prevention.
Oils from olive oil and castor oil; humectants from panthenol, propylene glycol and dipropylene glycol. No silicone, polymer or protein flag from the listed INCI.
Moisture because the formula is driven by smoothing/emollient/humectant conditioning and ceramide support, with no protein ingredients listed.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_conditioner_specs: Use snaps because the ingredient and claim pattern is moisture/smoothing/anti-frizz support with oils, panthenol, glycols and ceramide, not protein-building care.
Use snaps for coarse hair for the same moisture-oriented repair/anti-frizz positioning.

review.notes: Auto-approved after processed image and properties were both approved.

field_rationales.product.currency: Official Jean&Len shop price is in EUR.
field_rationales.product.image_url: Official brand-hosted front packshot shows the exact Jean&Len Rosemary Ginger Hair Mask Hydration jar.

field_rationales.category_specs.product_mask_specs: Cetearyl alcohol, isopropyl palmitate, oils, and conditioning quats indicate a medium mask rather than a light leave-in or very rich butter.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_mask_specs.weight: Cetearyl alcohol, isopropyl palmitate, oils, and conditioning quats indicate a medium mask rather than a light leave-in or very rich butter.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_mask_specs.concen: Rinse-out mask format plus concentrated conditioning base supports high concentration.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_mask_specs.ingred: INCI supports humectants, oils/emollients, hydrolyzed rice protein, and glucomannan polymer/film-former; no silicone flag because official page claims silicone-free and no obvious silicones appear in the listed INCI.
field_rationales.category_specs.product_mask_specs.balanc: Official positioning is moisture for dry, brittle, dull hair; INCI includes glycerin and emollients, with protein present but not dominant.

sources.title: OGX - ARGAN OIL OF MOROCCO Shampoo 385ml
sources.evidence: Retailer page identifies OGX Argan Oil of Morocco reconstructing shampoo, 385 ml, code C007813, web price 6.59 EUR, catalog price 7.66 EUR, and temporary out-of-stock status. It describes use for dry and damaged hair, sulfate-free positioning, argan oil, shine, softness, and daily-use/cleansing tags, but does not establish a dry-scalp route.

sources.title: OGX shampoo with restorative argan oil 385 ml
sources.evidence: Retailer page confirms the same OGX argan oil shampoo, 385 ml, one unit, manufacturer Johnson & Johnson, product number 11060, and supplies the selected product-only packshot.

sources.title: The 9 Best Drugstore Shampoos for Men, Starting at Just $8
sources.evidence: Independent editorial source lists OGX Renewing + Argan Oil of Morocco Hydrating Shampoo for shine/frizz, hydrating feel, argan-kernel oil, frizzy/damaged hair, and sulfate-free status. It says the shampoo cleanses the scalp but frames the benefit around hair smoothness, shine, and damage protection, not dry scalp, dandruff, irritation, or oily scalp treatment.

field_rationales.identifiers: Pharmacy295 URL exposes 022796976116, stored as barcode because it is a barcode-like product identifier; Pharmacy295 code C007813 and GPC product number 11060 are stored as retailer_sku.

field_rationales.product.category_key: Submission category is shampoo and all evidence describes shampoo.

field_rationales.category_specs.product_shampoo_specs: Reworked per reviewer instruction: dry/damaged hair and hydration/frizz claims are hair-length/condition claims, not proof of dry scalp. No source supports dandruff, irritated scalp, oily scalp, dry flakes, dry scalp, or clarifying/deep-cleansing routing. Therefore all thickness rows use shampoo_bucket normal, scalp_route balanced, and cleansing_intensity regular.
